# Install
```{r}
## One-time install
# install.packages("dartR")
# install.packages("BiocManager")
# install.packages("devtools")
# BiocManager::install(c("SNPRelate", "qvalue"))
# gl.install.vanilla.dartR()
```

# Import and check genlight object
```{r}
# import data
library(dartR)

gl <- gl.read.dart(
  filename = "Genetic Analysis/Report_DAc23-8280_SNP_2.csv",
  ind.metafile = "Genetic Analysis/ind_metrics_site.csv" # csv file which contains metrics associated with each individual (id, pop, sex, etc.)
)

# filter out Low Isles slope pale as we are not using the matching phenotypic data, filter mangrove coral as 1 individual is not valid for a population analysis
gl <- gl.drop.pop(gl, pop.list = "Low Isles slope pale")
gl <- gl.drop.pop(gl, pop.list = "Low Isles mangrove")
# filer out individuals that have been identified as outliers in the PCA RB F1, MO S30, MO S23, MO S24, RB F14, MO S26 - these individuals have also been removed from phenotype analysis
gl <- gl.drop.ind(gl, ind.list = c("89", "146", "139", "140", "102", "142"))

# to check genlight object is compliant
gl <- gl.compliance.check(gl)

# save(gl, file="gl_dartR.rdata")
```

# Filtering data
```{r}
## 1. FILTER SECONDARIES
# gl.report.secondaries(gl) # report
gl_f <- gl.filter.secondaries(gl, verbose = 3) # filtering
# this is standard, no threshold setc

## 2. FILTER REPRODUCIBILITY
# gl.report.reproducibility(gl_f) # report
gl_f <- gl.filter.reproducibility(gl_f, threshold = 0.99, verbose = 3) # filtering threshold 0.99

## 3. FILTER CALL RATE BY LOCI
# gl.report.callrate(gl_f, method = "loc") # report
gl_f <- gl.filter.callrate(gl_f, method = "loc", threshold = 0.80, verbose = 3) # filtering
# Note: this has been changed to 0.8 due to significant loss of data at higher thresholds
# This retains a good number of loci, 0.93 also seems appropriate, 0.95 seems to strict (only 50 loci remain)

## 4. FILTER CALL RATE BY INDIVIDUALS
# gl.report.callrate(gl_f, method = "ind") # report
gl_f <- gl.filter.callrate(gl_f, method = "ind", threshold = 0.80, verbose = 3) # filtering

## 5. FILTER READ DEPTH TO REMOVE LOW COVERAGE LOCI
# gl.report.rdepth(gl_f) # report
gl_f <- gl.filter.rdepth(gl_f, lower = 5, upper = 85, verbose = 3) # filtering
# selecting a threshold of 10% either side (data between the 10th and 90th percentile was kept)

## 6. FILTER MONOMORPHIC LOCI
# gl.report.monomorphs(gl_f) # report
gl_f <- gl.filter.monomorphs(gl_f, verbose = 3) # filtering
# Filtered prior to MAF as it was generating a warning about monomorphoc loci

## 7. FILTER Minor Allele Frequencies (MAF)
# gl.report.maf(gl_f) # report
gl_f <- gl.filter.maf(gl_f, threshold = 0.01, verbose = 3) # filtering

# filtering complete.

gl_f <- gl.recalc.metrics(gl_f) # re-calculate locus metadata now that filtering is complete
```

# Combined filtered data with unfiltered Long Bommie SNPS
```{r}
# No filtering for one pop

# Get the filtered Genlight object (gl_f) without Long Bommie - already removed by filtering so this line is unnecessary
# without_long_bommie = gl.drop.pop(gl_f, pop.list=c('Opal Long Bommie'))

# Grab Long Bommie from the original, unfiltered Genlight object (gl)
long_bommie <- gl.keep.pop(gl, pop.list = c("Opal Long Bommie"))

# Keep only the loci that are also present in filtered Genlight object (gl_f)
# Otherwise we can't recombine this data
long_bommie <- gl.keep.loc(long_bommie, locNames(gl_f))

# Combine all the filtered data with the unfiltered Long Bommie data
gl_f_with_long_bommie <- rbind(gl_f, long_bommie)

gl_f_with_long_bommie <- gl.compliance.check(gl_f_with_long_bommie) # get back missing metadata that rbind lost

gl_f_with_long_bommie <- gl.recalc.metrics(gl_f_with_long_bommie) # re-calculate locus metadata
```

# Test for clones - none under the threshold 0.45
```{r}
# 80% & 70% loci coverage
gl_grm <- gl.grm(gl_f, verbose = 3)

# This function calculates the mean probability of identity by state (IBS) across loci that would result from all the possible crosses of the individuals analyzed.
# IBD is calculated by an additive relationship matrix approach developed by Endelman and Jannink (2012)

# Represents a genomic relationship matrix (GRM) as a network
gl_grm_net <- gl.grm.network(gl_grm, gl_f, method = "mds", link.size = 2, relatedness_factor = 0.45) # decided on value between sibling/half sibling
```

# Impute missing data using the nearest neighbour function - needed for some downstream analyses (e.g. Fst)
```{r}
gl_f2 <- gl.impute(gl_f, method = "neighbour", verbose = 5)
# re-calculating the locus metrics after manipulation
gl_f2 <- gl.recalc.metrics(gl_f2)

# 2244 / (nInd(gl_f) * nLoc(gl_f)) # Percentage imputed
```

# Check for Symbiodiniaceae contamination - no symbiont sequences aligned with filtered data indicating no contamination
```{r}
# Download Symbiodiniaceae genomes here: sampgr.org.cn/index.php/download
# Download & install BLAST (needed to run analysis) https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/blast/executables/blast+/LATEST/
# select this file: ncbi-blast-2.14.1+.dmg
# Run the BLAST function for each genome
gl.blast(gl_f, "Symbiontgenomes/symC_scaffold_40.fasta", verbose = 5)
gl.blast(gl_f, "Symbiontgenomes/Symbiodinium_microadriacticum_genome.scaffold.fasta", verbose = 5)
gl.blast(gl_f, "Symbiontgenomes/symA3_scaffold_37.fasta", verbose = 5)
gl.blast(gl_f, "Symbiontgenomes/Fugacium_kawagutii_V3_genome_scaffold.fasta", verbose = 5)
gl.blast(gl_f, "Symbiontgenomes/Fugacium_kawagutii_V2_genome_Scaffolds.fasta", verbose = 5)
gl.blast(gl_f, "Symbiontgenomes/Fugacium_kawagutii_V1_genome_scaffold.fasta", verbose = 5)
gl.blast(gl_f, "Symbiontgenomes/Cladocopium_goreaui_Genome.Scaffolds.fasta", verbose = 5)
gl.blast(gl_f, "Symbiontgenomes/Breviolum_minutum.v1.0.genome.fa", verbose = 5)
```


#PCA
```{r}
library(adegenet)
source("default_theme.r")

# Switch between with/without long_bommie
pca_data <- gl_f
# pca_data = gl_f_with_long_bommie

# levels(pca_data$pop) = c(
#   "L1 Flat", "L1 Pale" ,"L2 Slope", "Low Isles slope pale",
#   "L2 Flat", "O2 Crest", "O2 Slope", "O3 Flat", "O3 Slope" ,
#   "T1 Slope", "T2 Slope", "O1 Slope"
# )

pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Low Isles flat", new = "L1 Flat")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Low Isles flat pale", new = "L1 Pale")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Low Isles Woody flat", new = "L2 Flat")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Low Isles slope", new = "L2 Slope")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Tongue Turtle Bay", new = "T2 Slope")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Tongue Blue Hole", new = "T1 Slope")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Opal Long Bommie", new = "O1 Slope")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Opal Mojo flat", new = "O2 Crest")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Opal Mojo slope", new = "O2 Slope")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Opal Rayban flat", new = "O3 Flat")
pca_data <- gl.rename.pop(pca_data, old = "Opal Rayban slope", new = "O3 Slope")

pcoa <- gl.pcoa(pca_data, verbose = 3)

# gl.pcoa.plot won't return the plot for us to work with
# We can tell it to save it in a temporary directory though
gl.pcoa.plot(pcoa, pca_data, pop.labels = "pop", ellipse = FALSE, save2tmp = TRUE)
# Now we need to get the list of repots it has saved and find the last plot
gl.list.reports()
# put the number of that plot here so we can finally get the plot and work with it
plot <- gl.print.reports(5)

# Remove the horizontal and vertical lines
plot$layers[[4]] <- NULL
plot$layers[[3]] <- NULL

# output the plot
plot +
  # stat_ellipse(type = "norm", level = 0.95) +
   xlim(-5,10)+ ylim(-10,10)+
  default_theme

ggsave("Genetic Analysis/plots/pca.png",width=6,height=5)
# ggsave("Genetic Analysis/plots/pca_longbommie.png", width = 6, height = 5)


###### To find out which samples are outliers ###### - this only needs to be run once

# Similar to how we get the plot, the results must be found by running gl.list.reports() and finding the latest Table number
pca_results <- gl.print.reports(16)

# Load the metadata so we can get Sample IDs
metadata <- read_csv("Genetic Analysis/ind_metrics_site.csv") %>%
  # Create a `ind` column to match the PCA output
  mutate(ind = as.character(id))


outliers <- pca_results %>%
  # Join the metadata so we can get Sample IDs
  left_join(metadata, "ind") %>%
  # We can see from the plot there are some outliers above PC2 = 4 and below PC2 = -4
  filter(PCoAy > 4 | PCoAy < -4)
```

# STRUCTURE analysis - suggests 1 population
```{r}
library(stringr) # for str_trunc

# gl.run.structure takes a genlight object and runs structureRun (https://rdrr.io/cran/dartR/man/gl.run.structure.html)
#     ↓↓
# structureRun expects a gtypes object in R and runs STRUCTURE from inside R (https://rdrr.io/cran/strataG/man/structure.html)
#     ↓↓
# STRUCTURE is a program external to R
# The program: ./STRUCTURE/structure
# Documentation: https://web.stanford.edu/group/pritchardlab/structure_software/release_versions/v2.3.4/structure_doc.pdf

# For this function to work, make sure that individual and population names have no spaces.
# make a copy od the data to make changes to
gl_for_structure <- gl_f
# Replace spaces in the population names with underscores
popNames(gl_for_structure) <- gsub(" ", "_", popNames(gl_for_structure))

# Structure truncates individual names at 11 characters. The function will fail if the names of individuals are not unique after truncation.
# Replace reef names with shorter
popNames(gl_for_structure) <- gsub("Low_Isles", "L", popNames(gl_for_structure))
popNames(gl_for_structure) <- gsub("Opal_Mojo", "M", popNames(gl_for_structure))
popNames(gl_for_structure) <- gsub("Opal_Rayban", "R", popNames(gl_for_structure))
popNames(gl_for_structure) <- gsub("Tongue", "T", popNames(gl_for_structure))
# Show what names shortened to 11 characters would be
str_trunc(popNames(gl_for_structure), 11, ellipsis = "")

# gl.run.structure below works but R has no way to capture stdout from it and
# there's a lot of output so it overflows the terminal and we can't ever get it
# The solution is to output gl_for_structure to file, and run gl.run.structure
# in another R session piping the output to a file
save(gl_for_structure, file = "gl_for_structure.RData")
# now run `Rscript structure_run.r > outputs/structure_output.txt` in terminal
# Now we load the results of gl.run.structure from structure_run.r
load("structure_run.RData") # loads a variable called sr

# This is the code that's now run inside structure_run.r
# sr <- gl.run.structure(
#   gl_for_structure,
#   burnin = 10000,
#   numreps = 10000,
#   save2tmp = TRUE,

#   # Documentation of options here: https://rdrr.io/cran/strataG/man/structure.html#heading-2
#   k.range = 1:10,
#   num.k.rep = 5, # number of replicates for each value in k.range.

#   # The location of the external STRUCUTRE program to run
#   exec = "./STRUCTURE/structure"
# )

# Calculate Evanno metrics
ev <- gl.evanno(sr)
ev
# Creates Evanno plots
evanno_plots <- gl.evanno(sr)

source("../.r")

# delta.k
evanno_plots$plots$delta.k + default_theme
ggsave("Genetic Analysis/plots/evanno_delta_k.png")

# ln.pk
evanno_plots$plots$ln.pk + default_theme
ggsave("Genetic Analysis/plots/evanno_ln_pk.png")

# ln.ppk
evanno_plots$plots$ln.ppk + default_theme
ggsave("Genetic Analysis/plots/evanno_ln_ppk.png")

# mean.ln.k
evanno_plots$plots$mean.ln.k + default_theme
ggsave("Genetic Analysis/plots/evanno_mean_ln_k.png")
```

# ADMIXUTRE analysis
```{r}
# Converting genlight to PLINK format
gl2plink(gl_f, plink_path = "./PLINK", bed_file = TRUE, verbose = 5, outpath = "./PLINK")
# The file "PLINK/gl_plink.bed" has been created and exported out of R for use in shell
# Note PLINK folder moved up one level in folders as it does not like spaces in file names

# create ind2pop.txt needed for pong
ind2pop <- pop(gl_f) # Get population names for each individual
levels(ind2pop) <- gsub(" ", "", levels(ind2pop)) # Remove spaces from factor levels
write.table(ind2pop, "Genetic Analysis/outputs/ind2pop.txt", row.names = FALSE, col.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
# The file "ind2pop.txt" has been created and exported out of R for use in shell
# Saved to outputs folder in Genetic Analysis - not saved in PLINK folder

# In terminal run admixture using the exported files from above - https://speciationgenomics.github.io/ADMIXTURE/
# Use ADMIXTURE/ADMIXTURE.sh to run ADMIXTURE and pong (plots)

```


# Genetic distance matrix between populations
```{r}
# Genetic distance between two individuals or populations is a measure of their genetic dissimilarity. large value = very dissimilar, small value = similar, 0 = identical genetic profiles
# gl.dist.ind() or gl.dist.pop()
# Note: Method = “euclidean” is severely affected by missing values and should be used only for complete data. Therefore, use "gl_f2" which has filled in any missing data via the nearest neighbour function

Dist_pop <- gl.dist.pop(gl_f2, method = "euclidean")
Dist_pop

# Heat map
svg("Genetic Analysis/plots/heatmap.svg")
gl.plot.heatmap(Dist_pop,
  key = TRUE, keysize = 1.5, key.title = NA, key.ylab = NA,
  cexRow = 1, cexCol = 1, margins = c(10, 10), srtCol = 45
)
dev.off()

# Calculate Fst values - suggests only Low Isles Mangrove is different
# Fst is a measure of population differentiation due to genetic structure. An Fst value greater than 0.15 can be considered as significant in differentiating populations (Frankham et al., 2002).
Fst <- gl.fst.pop(gl_f2, nboots = 999) # note: don't need to include nboots if I'm not interested in p-values
Fst

write.csv(Fst$Fsts, "Genetic Analysis/outputs/Fst.csv")
write.csv(Fst$Pvalues, "Genetic Analysis/outputs/FstPvalues.csv")
```

# Run 'popgen' (snpReady) for genetic characterisation
```{r}
library(snpReady)

# Get current populations from gl_f
populations <- as.matrix(pop(gl_f))

# Run popgen with subgroups equal to my 'populations'
popgen_output <- popgen(
  as.matrix(gl_f),
  subgroups = populations
)

means <- NULL

for (pop in names(popgen_output$bygroup)) {
  pop_data <- popgen_output$bygroup[[pop]]
  print(pop) # prints name of the 'population'
  # print(pop_data$Genotypes)
  # print(pop_data$Markers)
  # print(pop_data$Population)
  # print(pop_data$Variability)

  # calculate metrics of interest - we are using genetic diversity (GD)
  means <- rbind(means, data.frame(
    pop = pop,
    p = mean(pop_data$Markers$p), # allele frequency (frequency of dominant allele - p)
    q = mean(pop_data$Markers$q), # allele frequency (frequency of recessive allele - q)
    MAF = mean(pop_data$Markers$MAF), # minor allele frequency
    He = mean(pop_data$Markers$He), # observed heterozygosity
    Ho = mean(pop_data$Markers$Ho), # expected heterozygosity
    GD = mean(pop_data$Markers$GD), # Nei's genetic diversity
    PIC = mean(pop_data$Markers$PIC), # polymorphic information count
    Miss = mean(pop_data$Markers$Miss), # missing rate,
    n = nrow(pop_data$Genotypes) #gives sample number for each group
  ))
}

print(means)
write.csv(means, "Genetic Analysis/outputs/GeneticDiversity.csv")
```
